---
title: "Exercises"
---

End-of-chapter exercise sections follow the same pattern across every
book in this project. Each problem sits in an `::: {.exercise}` block
and is immediately followed by an `::: {.solution}` block. The solution
block can be left empty — readers fill it in themselves — and the HTML
build collapses it behind a "Solution" disclosure.

## Style guide

- **Difficulty.** Match the difficulty of the past-exam questions you
  have on hand. State numerical assumptions explicitly so the problem is
  pen-and-paper solvable.
- **Self-contained.** Restate any non-obvious notation or distributions
  the problem depends on. Don't assume the reader has just finished the
  current chapter — many will land here from the table of contents.
- **One result per box.** If a problem has two parts that are usefully
  separated, split them into two exercises rather than nesting.

## Worked example

The following exercise is shown with a worked solution. Most of the
time the `.solution` block is left empty.

::: {.exercise}

Let $X \sim \text{Exp}(\lambda)$. Compute the survival probability
$P(X > a + b \mid X > a)$ and identify the memoryless property.
:::

::: {.solution}

By the definition of conditional probability,
$$P(X > a + b \mid X > a) = \frac{P(X > a + b)}{P(X > a)}
  = \frac{e^{-\lambda(a + b)}}{e^{-\lambda a}} = e^{-\lambda b}
  = P(X > b).$$
The conditional tail probability depends only on $b$, not on the elapsed
time $a$ — the exponential is the only continuous distribution with this
property.
:::

## Unsolved exercises

Below is the standard pattern: problem in an `.exercise` block, an
empty `.solution` block to be filled in by the reader.

::: {.exercise}

For $X \sim \text{Binomial}(n, p)$, derive $E[X]$ and $\text{Var}(X)$ by
writing $X = \sum_{i=1}^n Y_i$ as a sum of i.i.d.\ Bernoulli indicators.
:::

::: {.solution}

:::

::: {.exercise}

A Markov chain on $\{0, 1\}$ has transition probabilities
$P(0 \to 1) = \alpha$ and $P(1 \to 0) = \beta$. Find the stationary
distribution in closed form.
:::

::: {.solution}

:::

## Contributing solutions

Exercise solutions are intentionally left empty in the chapter source so
the books remain useful as study material. If you have worked solutions
you would like to share, please open a discussion on the repository's
[Issues page](../../../issues) — paste your working in a comment, attach
a notebook or a photo, and the project maintainers will review and
merge solutions that fit the chapter's style.

## Sources

Edit this list per-chapter. End the section with the lecture notes and
textbooks that inspired the exercise set.
